welcome.title: How can I help you today?
welcome.placeholder: Describe what you'd like to do...
welcome.quickPromptCreate: Create a new file for me
welcome.quickPromptCrunch: Help me analyze and process data
welcome.quickPromptOrganize: Help me organize my files and folders
welcome.quickPromptEmail: Help me use Chrome to summarize the new emails from the past three days in my Gmail and NetEase Mail. Note that the saved accounts already include the full email suffix. Therefore, if the email suffix is already pre-filled on the webpage or in a screenshot, do not enter it again, to avoid login failure. Also, first check whether the corresponding account credentials are saved. If the username or password for a given email service is not saved, you can skip that email account.
welcome.quickPromptPapers:
Please help me use Chrome to search for and summarize papers related to [Agent] within two days.
Source websites:
1. HuggingFace Daily Papers. Please include the vote information and a brief summary. Note that it may not include papers in the weekend, so you may need to check the papers in previous days. But make sure that there is a total of two days.
welcome.quickPromptNotion: Help me research three representative survey papers related to agents, and add them under a Notion page titled "Agent Survey". For each paper, include the title, authors, publication venue/year, and a brief summary of the main contributions.
welcome.apiNotConfigured: API not configured yet. Please go to Settings to set up your API provider and key.

memory.title: Memory
memory.description: Global core memory stores stable preferences, while workspace experience memory stores project-specific multi-turn knowledge.
memory.toggleHint: Turning this off stops future ingestion and auto-recall, but keeps existing records.
memory.searchDescription: Search memory by current workspace or globally, then inspect summaries and raw excerpts.
memory.noSelection: Select a result to inspect the full summary and source excerpt.
memory.maintenanceDescription: Rebuild experience memory for the current workspace or clear saved data.
memory.rebuildConfirm: Rebuild experience memory for the current workspace? This rescans historical sessions in that workspace.
memory.clearWorkspaceConfirm: Clear experience memory for the current workspace? This cannot be undone.
memory.clearCoreConfirm: Clear all global core memory? This cannot be undone.

api.firstRunSubtitle: Configure a provider to start using Open Cowork
api.settingsSubtitle: Manage provider, key, and model settings
api.currentSetSavingHint: "Save Settings" updates only the currently selected config set.
api.unsavedCurrentSetHint: Current config set has unsaved changes.
api.unsavedSwitchPrompt: Current config set has unsaved changes. Choose an action before switching to "{{name}}".
api.configSetSaveAsPrompt: Enter a name for the new config set
api.configSetRenamePrompt: Enter a new name for this config set
api.configSetLimitReached: You can save up to {{count}} config sets
api.providerDescription: Choose the provider family and the broad protocol style for this workspace.
api.apiKeyDescription: Saved locally and scoped to the active configuration set.
api.selectProtocol: Select the compatible protocol for the service
api.enterOllamaUrl: Enter the Ollama OpenAI-compatible service URL
api.enterModelId: Enter model ID, e.g., deepseek-chat, kimi-k2-thinking, gpt-5.4
api.contextWindowHint: Leave empty for auto-detection. Override only if the model reports incorrect limits.
api.saveFailed: Failed to save settings. Please try again.
api.configRequiredActiveSet: The current config set does not have usable credentials. Finish setup in API Settings first.
api.refreshModelsFailed: Failed to refresh models. Check the URL or service status.
api.discoverLocalOllamaHint: Enter your Ollama endpoint first. Models will be fetched automatically, and manual entry is only needed if discovery fails.
api.localOllamaDiscovered: Found local Ollama with {{count}} available model(s).
api.localOllamaNotFound: No local Ollama service was found. Start it with `ollama serve` and try again.
api.localOllamaNoModels: Found a local Ollama service, but it does not currently expose any models.
api.localOllamaModelUnavailable: Found local Ollama, but model {{model}} could not complete a minimal inference request.
api.localOllamaModelLoading: Found local Ollama, but the model is still loading into memory. Large models may take up to a minute.
api.enableThinkingHint: Show Claude's thinking process step by step. This provides more transparency but may increase token usage.
api.enableThinkingOllamaHint: Most Ollama models do not support thinking mode. Enable only if your model explicitly supports it.
api.testSuccessNeedSave: Connection test passed. Save settings before starting a session.

testError.missing_base_url: Base URL is required for this provider
testError.not_found: Endpoint not found. Check the Base URL
testError.server_error: Service error. Please try again later
testError.ollama_not_running: Cannot connect to Ollama. Please make sure Ollama is running (ollama serve).
testError.ollama_loading: Ollama is loading the model into memory. Large models may take 30–120 seconds. Please wait and try again.

guidance.commonSetupsHint: Review or apply a recommended setup
guidance.preferProviderTab: Detected {{service}}. This service is usually easier to configure from the dedicated {{provider}} tab.
guidance.protocolLooksGood: Detected {{service}}. Your current protocol already matches the recommended {{recommendedProtocol}} setup.
guidance.genericBaseUrlHint: This looks like a custom {{recommendedProtocol}}-compatible endpoint. Recommended base URL format: {{baseUrl}}. Example model: {{model}}.

errorHints.emptyProbeGeneric: The endpoint returned an empty probe response. This usually means the selected protocol or model is incompatible with the service.
errorHints.emptyProbeDetected: {{service}} returned an empty probe response. Try the recommended {{recommendedProtocol}} setup and verify the model ID for that endpoint.
errorHints.emptyProbePreferProvider: {{service}} returned an empty probe response. Try the dedicated {{provider}} tab instead of configuring it as a generic custom endpoint.
errorHints.probeMismatchGeneric: The endpoint responded, but not in the format expected by this probe. This usually points to a protocol or model compatibility mismatch.
errorHints.probeMismatchDetected: {{service}} responded with an unexpected probe format. Try the recommended {{recommendedProtocol}} setup and verify the model ID for that endpoint.

openrouter.name: OpenRouter
openrouter.note: Prefer the dedicated OpenRouter provider tab unless you are debugging a custom relay.

deepseek.name: DeepSeek API
deepseek.note: Use OpenAI-compatible mode and copy the exact model ID from DeepSeek docs.

kimi.name: Kimi Coding
kimi.note: Use the coding endpoint with Anthropic-compatible mode. Double-check the exact coding model ID from Moonshot docs.

glm.name: GLM / BigModel (Anthropic route)
glm.note: Use the Anthropic-compatible route when the endpoint path includes /api/anthropic.

ollama.name: Ollama
ollama.note: Prefer the dedicated Ollama provider tab for local model discovery and refresh.

gemini.name: Gemini custom endpoint
gemini.note: Use Gemini-compatible mode and the exact model ID exposed by the endpoint.

minimax.name: MiniMax
minimax.note: Use OpenAI-compatible mode unless your MiniMax gateway documents another protocol.

genericOpenAI.name: Generic OpenAI-compatible
genericOpenAI.note: Use this for custom relays, gateways, or inference servers that mimic the OpenAI API.

api.renameInlineHint: Edit directly. Press Enter or blur to save automatically
api.configSetCloneSourceMissing: Could not find a config set to clone
api.configSetSystemDeleteForbidden: The default config set cannot be deleted
api.configSetKeepOne: Keep at least one config set

advisory.not_deep_verified: The endpoint is reachable and the selected model is listed, but no live inference was performed yet.
advisory.model_loading: The endpoint is reachable, but the model may still be loading into memory.

fix.dns_resolve_failed: Cannot resolve hostname {{host}}. Please check if the URL is correct.
fix.tcp_connect_failed: Cannot connect to {{host}}. Please check your network or firewall settings.
fix.tls_handshake_failed: TLS handshake failed. This may be caused by a certificate issue or proxy interference.
fix.missing_api_key: API Key is missing or invalid. Please check your key.
fix.auth_invalid_key: API Key is invalid or expired. Please check if the key is correct.
fix.auth_endpoint_not_found: API endpoint not found. Please check if the Base URL is correct.
fix.auth_request_failed: Authentication request failed. Please check your configuration.
fix.model_network_error: Could not reach model {{model}}. Check your network, VPN, or proxy settings.
fix.model_rate_limited: Model {{model}} is currently rate limited or out of quota. Please try again later.
fix.model_request_failed: Model {{model}} request failed on the provider side. Please try again later.
fix.model_unavailable: Model {{model}} is not available. Please verify the model name.
fix.ollama_no_models_loaded: The endpoint is reachable, but it did not return any models. Pull a model in Ollama first.
fix.ollama_model_not_listed: Model {{model}} is not in the endpoint model list. Pick a detected model or switch to manual entry.
fix.ollama_model_loading: Model {{model}} may still be loading. Try the deep check again in a moment.
fix.model_cold_start: The model may still be loading into GPU/CPU memory. Large models (7B+) can take 30–120 seconds on first use. Try again shortly.

sandbox.title: Sandbox Environment
sandbox.wslDesc: Run commands in WSL2 Linux environment for better isolation and security on Windows.
sandbox.limaDesc: Run commands in Lima VM for better isolation and security on macOS.
sandbox.nativeDesc: Commands run directly on your system (Linux native mode).
sandbox.notReadyStatus: Sandbox enabled but not fully configured
sandbox.disabledStatus: Sandbox disabled - commands run directly on system
sandbox.wslInstallHint: Install WSL2 for better isolation. Run this command in PowerShell as Administrator:
sandbox.limaInstallHint: Install Lima for better isolation. Run this command in Terminal:
sandbox.linuxNative: Linux runs commands natively without additional sandboxing.
sandbox.helpText1: Sandbox provides an isolated environment for running commands safely.
sandbox.helpText2: WSL2 (Windows) or Lima VM (macOS) is required for full sandbox support.
sandbox.setupSubtitle: First run requires configuring a secure execution environment.
sandbox.configuredSuccess: Sandbox configured. Code can now be executed safely.
sandbox.nativeFallbackSuccess: Using the native system environment for command execution.
sandbox.syncExplanation: Syncing project files to the isolated sandbox for secure code execution.
sandbox.wslNotDetectedMessage: WSL2 not detected, using native mode
sandbox.limaNotDetectedMessage: Lima not detected, using native mode
sandbox.setupDetailSandboxDisabled: Using native execution mode (sandbox disabled in settings)
sandbox.setupDetailInstallWsl: Install WSL2 for better sandbox isolation
sandbox.setupDetailInstallNodeWsl: Please install Node.js manually in WSL
sandbox.setupDetailInstallDeps: Installing markitdown, pypdf, pdfplumber for PDF/PPTX skills
sandbox.setupDetailInstallLima: Install Lima for better sandbox isolation (brew install lima)
sandbox.setupDetailCreateLima: First run requires image download and may take a few minutes
sandbox.setupDetailStartLima: VM startup may take a few minutes
sandbox.setupDetailInstallNodeLima: Installing Node.js runtime in Lima VM
sandbox.setupDetailInstallPythonLima: Installing Python runtime in Lima VM
sandbox.syncDetailWsl: Copying project files to isolated WSL environment
sandbox.syncDetailLima: Copying project files to isolated Lima environment
sandbox.syncDetailFallback: Falling back to direct access mode (less secure)

logs.title: Application Logs
logs.description: View and export diagnostic bundles for debugging. Logs rotate automatically after 10MB, and exports also include recent session and error summaries.
logs.inventoryDescription: Current log inventory and storage footprint.
logs.recentDescription: Recent application logs available for inspection. You can still export a diagnostics bundle even when no log file exists yet.
logs.directoryDescription: Current on-disk location for application logs.
logs.actionsDescription: Export a diagnostics bundle, reveal the log directory, or clear log files.
logs.enableDevLogsDesc: Record detailed logs for debugging. Disable to reduce disk usage.
logs.clearConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear all log files? This action cannot be undone.
logs.helpText1: The exported diagnostics bundle includes app logs, a redacted runtime snapshot, and metadata-only session/error summaries without message bodies by default.
logs.helpText2: Sharing that bundle usually lets us narrow issues down much faster, whether they come from the UI, tool execution, or local environment.

sidebar.noTasksHint: Start a new chat to begin building, researching, or editing files.
sidebar.localTasks: These tasks run locally and aren't synced across devices.
sidebar.batchDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} conversations? This cannot be undone.

chat.startFailed: Failed to start session. Please try again.

context.stepsWillShow: Steps will show as the task unfolds.

schedule.autoTitleChangedHint: Prompt changed. The title will be regenerated after saving.
schedule.autoTitleUnchangedHint: If the prompt stays the same, the existing title will be kept.
schedule.autoTitleEditingChanged: After saving, the title will be regenerated from the prompt with a [Scheduled Task] prefix.
schedule.autoTitleEditingUnchanged: After saving, the title will be generated with a [Scheduled Task] prefix.
schedule.autoTitleCreating: After saving, the title will be generated with a [Scheduled Task] prefix and a model summary.
schedule.promptPlaceholder: Prompt to run automatically at the scheduled time
schedule.cwdPlaceholder: Working directory (defaults to the current workspace)
schedule.executionTimeHint: New schedules prefer multi-slot timing; legacy interval rules can still be edited.
schedule.legacyIntervalNotice: This task comes from a legacy fixed-interval rule and will keep that mode when saved.
schedule.legacyStartTimeLabel: Choose the start time for the legacy interval rule
schedule.dailyHint: Run automatically at these times every day.
schedule.weeklyHint: Run automatically on the selected weekdays and time slots.
schedule.listHint: Disabling only blocks future automatic runs. Already started sessions must be stopped manually from the session list.
schedule.dailyTimesRequired: Select at least one daily time slot
schedule.weeklyTimesRequired: Select at least one weekly time slot
schedule.nextRunCalculationFailed: The current rule cannot calculate the next run time
schedule.futureTimeRequired: Execution time must be later than now. Use Run now if you want to trigger it immediately.
schedule.taskMissing: Task no longer exists or was deleted
schedule.noSessionToStop: This task has no execution session to stop
schedule.sessionNotRunning: This task does not currently have a running session
schedule.stopRunTitleActive: Stop the latest execution session for this task
schedule.stopRunTitleIdle: This task does not currently have a running session
schedule.previewSelectAtLeastOne: Choose at least one valid time slot
schedule.previewAutoFind: The system will automatically choose the next run time: {{value}}

remote.title: Remote Control
remote.feishuDesc: Create an app in Feishu Open Platform, then fill in the credentials here.
remote.policyPairingDesc: Users must enter a pairing code.
remote.slackDesc: Create a Slack app, install it to your workspace, then fill in the credentials here.
remote.slackSocketModeDesc: Connect outward to Slack. No public IP required.
remote.connectionDesc: Choose how Feishu servers communicate with your computer.
remote.longConnectionDesc: The app connects outward to Feishu servers. No public IP or ngrok required.
remote.webhookDesc: Feishu pushes messages to your server. A publicly reachable address is required.
remote.longConnectionHint: In Feishu Open Platform, go to Events & Callbacks > Event Configuration and switch the subscription mode to long connection.
remote.defaultWorkingDirectoryHint: This is the default directory for AI commands. You can also override it in a message with `[cwd:path]`.
remote.autoApproveSafeToolsDesc: Automatically allow safe tools such as file reads and browser operations.
remote.quickStartStep1: Create an app in Feishu Open Platform and add the Bot capability.
remote.quickStartStep2: Copy the App ID and App Secret into the fields above.
remote.quickStartStep3: Enable these permissions in the permission settings:
remote.quickStartStep4: In Feishu's Event Configuration, choose the long connection event mode.
remote.quickStartStep6: Publish the app, then click Start service.
remote.pairingGuideStep1: Open Feishu and find the bot
remote.pairingGuideStep2: Send any message to initiate pairing
remote.pairingGuideStep4: Done — the user can now chat with the bot
remote.waitingForPairing: Waiting for users to initiate pairing...

permission.warning: This action may modify your system. Review carefully.

toolDescriptions.write: Write to files on your system
toolDescriptions.edit: Edit existing files on your system
toolDescriptions.list_directory: List directory contents in the workspace
toolDescriptions.execute_command: Execute shell commands in the workspace

sudo.description: The following command requires administrator privileges:
sudo.warning: Your password will only be used for this command and will not be stored.
